title: meta-os.config.json — instance estate configuration
description: Schema for a meta-os instance's estate config (meta-os.config.json): the estate knowledge an adopter declares — variable prefixes, backlog mirrors, and memory topology. This is the FRAMEWORK level: valid keys, valid enum values, and defaults, carrying ZERO values. Deployment concerns (instanceRoot, frameworkRoot, github.*, auth, corsOrigins, claudeHome, dataDir) live in the dashboard's instance.config.json, not here. Every key is optional; omitting the file yields default behaviour.

vars.description: Reusable string prefixes. ${name} in any config value (or a projects/*.md 'path:') expands to these, so a repo-root move is a one-line change.

backlogs.description: Backlog mirrors this instance tracks, one per space/project.

space.description: Space/project key (e.g. the tracker project id).

path.description: Backlog source — a per-item raw/ directory (post-migration) or a legacy backlog.json. ${var} expands.

memory.description: Configurable memory topology. Omit the whole block for the default: the instance's own memory/{raw,wiki,output} as the sole canon root plus vaults/* as federated mounts (byte-for-byte the prior behaviour). ${var} expands in paths. A broken/dangling path is skipped and reported, never fatal.

roots.description: Canon roots — counted AS the raw->wiki->output pipeline (promotable). Each declares a layout.

layout.description: flat = {raw,wiki,output} at the root. tier/project = {raw,wiki,output}/<project> — favours cross-project browsing on a directly-read root. project/tier = <project>/{raw,wiki,output} — favours per-project ownership and clean extraction, and is REQUIRED when the root is federated one-symlink-per-project (a project must map to a single mountable directory).

federated.description: Federated mounts — surfaced in the graph for navigation/context, NEVER counted as canon.
